headline: 'Trump would benefit from rereading Obama's 2015 speech on Iran'
description: The US president belatedly realized that the military option carried significant risks. He did not rely on his administration's recognized experts on Iran, notes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

headline: Population's cadmium overexposure requires urgent action, French food agency tells government
French conservatives to vote on whether to hold a presidential primary
'In Paris, the ideal of urban transformation clearly resonated with voters'
description: Emmanuel Grégoire, the Socialist mayor-elect at the head of a left-wing and environmentalist coalition, now faces two main challenges: responding to the anxiety of Parisians who fear being driven out of their city by the high cost of living and pursuing a policy of solidarity with Greater Paris, writes economist Frédéric Gilli.

headline: Algerian President Abdelmadjid Tebboune strengthens his grip on power
description: On Wednesday, the Algerian Parliament is set to adopt a constitutional revision that increases the president's powers, particularly over judicial appointments and elections.

headline: From afar, American students in France feel the shockwaves of Trump
description: As academic freedom declines in the US, some young people are choosing France to pursue master's and doctoral degrees. Yet, between delayed scholarships, intrusive questions and stereotypes, their experience remains shaken by the latest developments in their country.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

Emmanuel Grégoire's long run to Paris City Hall
description: The next mayor of Paris, who will be officially elected by the city council on Sunday, will now step into the spotlight after years in the shadow of his political mentors.

headline: TotalEnergies bows to Trump administration, dropping two offshore wind projects
description: The French company is ending two offshore wind turbine programs off the coasts of New York and North Carolina, totaling 4 gigawatts of power. It will receive a $928 million reimbursement on the condition that the funds are reinvested in hydrocarbon projects.

headline: Trump steps back from his own Iran ultimatum
description: The US president postponed his deadline for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, claiming talks are underway with the regime, which Iranian officials deny.
byline: Claire Gatinois,  Ghazal Golshiri and  Gilles Paris
